**1,000,000,000,000** (**one trillion** on the short scale; **one billion** on the long scale; one thousand [billion](/wiki/1,000,000,000 "1,000,000,000"); one million [million](/wiki/1,000,000 "1,000,000")) is the [natural number](/wiki/Natural_number "Natural number") following [999,999,999,999](/wiki/100,000,000,000#900,000,000,000_to_999,999,999,999 "100,000,000,000") and preceding 1,000,000,000,001. It is known as 10 kharab, 1000 arab, or 1 lakh crore in the [Indian numbering system](/wiki/Indian_numbering_system "Indian numbering system").

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1,000,000,000,000&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The word **trillion** is borrowed from French *trillion*, which is from *tri-* ("three") + *-illion*. It can be abbreviated as **tril**, **tln**, **tn**, or simply **t**. Originally in [British English](/wiki/British_English "British English"), the word "trillion" referred to [million million millions](/wiki/Quintillion "Quintillion") (while "billion" referred to million millions, which is equal to trillion in [American English](/wiki/American_English "American English")). However, to many people, the word refers to one thousand billion (1,000,000,000,000) in the present day.

## Properties and usage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1,000,000,000,000&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Properties and usage")]

1,000,000,000,000 has the following properties and usage:

### In mathematics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1,000,000,000,000&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: In mathematics")]

* It is [even](/wiki/Even_number "Even number"), [composite](/wiki/Composite_number "Composite number"), [abundant](/wiki/Abundant_number "Abundant number"), [polite](/wiki/Polite_number "Polite number"), [practical](/wiki/Practical_number "Practical number"), [frugal](/wiki/Frugal_number "Frugal number"), and [regular](/wiki/Regular_number "Regular number"). It is also a [perfect power](/wiki/Perfect_power "Perfect power") and a [harshad number](/wiki/Harshad_number "Harshad number").
* It is written in [scientific notation](/wiki/Scientific_notation "Scientific notation") as **1 × 1012** or **1012** (**1E+12** or simply **1E12** in [E notation](/wiki/Scientific_notation#E_notation "Scientific notation")).
* It is the [square](/wiki/Square_(algebra) "Square (algebra)") of [1,000,000](/wiki/1,000,000 "1,000,000"), the [cube](/wiki/Cube_(algebra) "Cube (algebra)") of [10,000](/wiki/10,000 "10,000"), the [fourth power](/wiki/Fourth_power "Fourth power") of [1,000](/wiki/1000_(number) "1000 (number)"), and the [sixth power](/wiki/Sixth_power "Sixth power") of [100](/wiki/100 "100").

* It has an [Euler totient](/wiki/Euler%27s_totient_function "Euler's totient function") of 400,000,000,000, and an [aliquot sum](/wiki/Aliquot_sum "Aliquot sum") of 1,499,694,822,171.

### In science

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1,000,000,000,000&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: In science")]

* For [physical quantities](/wiki/Physical_quantities "Physical quantities"), the [metric prefix](/wiki/Metric_prefix "Metric prefix") **tera** (symbol **T**) indicates 1,000,000,000,000 times the base unit. For example, 1012 [bits](/wiki/Bit "Bit") of data is called a **terabit**, 1012 [joules](/wiki/Joule "Joule") of energy is called a **terajoule**, and 1012 [watts](/wiki/Watt "Watt") of power is called a **terawatt**.
* The computing performance of 1012 [floating point operations per second](/wiki/Floating_point_operations_per_second "Floating point operations per second") is known as **terascale computing**. It is the performance of several [supercomputers](/wiki/Supercomputer "Supercomputer"), such as [NEC SX-4](/wiki/NEC_SX-4 "NEC SX-4"),[[3]](#cite_note-3) Intel's [ASCI Red](/wiki/ASCI_Red "ASCI Red"), and [iPhone 15 Pro](/wiki/IPhone_15_Pro "IPhone 15 Pro") September 2023 [A17 Pro](/wiki/A17_Pro "A17 Pro") processor.

## Sense of scale

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1,000,000,000,000&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Sense of scale")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4e/Visualisation_1_Trillion.svg/500px-Visualisation_1_Trillion.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Visualisation_1_Trillion.svg)

Visualization of powers of ten from one to one trillion

Some comparisons below give a sense of how large 1,000,000,000,000 (1012) is, according to current evidence.

### Time

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1,000,000,000,000&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Time")]

* 1012 seconds—a [terasecond](/wiki/Terasecond "Terasecond")—is about 31 thousand years. [Homo sapiens](/wiki/Homo_sapiens "Homo sapiens") were living as 'hunter-gatherers' 1012 seconds ago.
* 1012 minutes (about 1.9 million years) ago, the human species [Homo erectus](/wiki/Homo_erectus "Homo erectus") was in existence.
* 1012 hours (about 114 million years) ago, [Late Cretaceous](/wiki/Late_Cretaceous "Late Cretaceous") dinosaurs dominated the land, and [giant squids](/wiki/Giant_squid "Giant squid") thrived in the oceans.
* 1012 days (about 2.74 billion years) ago, Earth was an alien world where only single-celled [microorganisms](/wiki/Microorganism "Microorganism") existed.
* 1012 months (about 83 billion years) ago, the universe itself did not exist, as it was created only 13.787 billion years ago. 1012 months from now, the Moon may collide with the Earth or be torn apart to form an [orbital ring](/wiki/Orbital_ring "Orbital ring"), if both have not already been destroyed.
* 1012 years—a [teraannus](/wiki/Teraannus "Teraannus")—is 70 times the [age of the universe](/wiki/Age_of_the_universe "Age of the universe").

### Distance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1,000,000,000,000&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Distance")]

* 1012 inches is about 15 million miles (24 million kilometres), approximately 1/6th the distance from the Earth to the Sun.
* 1012 metres (a [terametre](/wiki/Terametre "Terametre")) is over six times the distance from the Earth to the Sun. It is the estimated diameter of [Betelgeuse](/wiki/Betelgeuse "Betelgeuse"), a red supergiant star.
* 1012 kilometres (a [petametre](/wiki/Petametre "Petametre")) is about 1/10th a [light-year](/wiki/Light-year "Light-year").
* A light-year is approximately 5,878,600,000,000 miles.

### Area

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1,000,000,000,000&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Area")]

* 1012 square inches is approximately the total area of [Saint Lucia](/wiki/Saint_Lucia "Saint Lucia"), an island country in the eastern Caribbean.
* 1012 square feet is larger than the total area of [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal").
* 1012 square metres is approximately the total area of [Ethiopia](/wiki/Ethiopia "Ethiopia"), the 26th largest country on Earth.
* 1012 square kilometres is almost 2,000 times the Earth's total surface area.

### Volume

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1,000,000,000,000&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Volume")]

* If 1012 cubic metres of water were spread evenly over the Moon's surface, it would create a layer of water approximately 2.6 centimetres deep.
* 1012 grains of sand would occupy a volume of about 0.5 cubic metres.

### Economy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1,000,000,000,000&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Economy")]

* [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain")'s GDP in 2024 is approximately $1.72 trillion.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

### Nature

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=1,000,000,000,000&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Nature")]

* An average-sized mountain weighs about 1012 kilograms.
* [Andromeda Galaxy](/wiki/Andromeda_Galaxy "Andromeda Galaxy"), Milky Way's neighbor, contains about 1012 stars.
* It is estimated that there are around 2 trillion galaxies in the [observable universe](/wiki/Observable_universe "Observable universe").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)
* There were around 3 trillion trees on Earth in 2015.[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* Scientists have estimated that the number of fish in the ocean is around 3.5 trillion.[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* The surface of the human body houses roughly 1012 bacteria.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
